paper.title_en: Measurement of $╬×^-/\bar╬×^{+}$ production in jets from $Z$ boson decays with the DELPHI open data
paper.abstract_en: The $╬×^{-}/\bar╬×^{+}$ baryon production in jets provides a probe of double-strangeness creation and baryon formation in the non-perturbative quantum chromodynamics (QCD) process. This study measures the $╬×^{-}/\bar╬×^{+}$ production rates and momentum spectra in energy-ranked jets using 3.2 million $Z\to\text{hadrons}$ events from the DELPHI open data collected from 1992 to 1995. Jets are reconstructed with the Durham algorithm with $y_{\text{cut}}=0.005$, and quark- and gluon-enriched jet samples are obtained from the energy ordering of jets in three-jet events. The average $╬×^{-}/\bar╬×^{+}$ production in energy-ranked jets are measured. The ratio of $╬×^{-}/\bar╬×^{+}$ production rates in gluon and quark jets relative to the mean charged-particle multiplicity is derived from the measurements. We obtain $R^{╬×^{-}}{g/q}=1.21\pm0.18\mathrm{(stat.)}\pm0.26\mathrm{(syst.)}$, consistent with the JETSET expectation and the OPAL results for $R^{K_S^0}_{g/q}$ and $R^╬ø_{g/q}$ within uncertainties. This study is the first measurement of $╬×^{-}/\bar╬×^{+}$ production in energy-ranked jets in $Z$ boson decays.
